title: 11 Herbal Creams For Acute Bronchitis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan for acute bronchitis. But let's talk about herbal teas, which are a more traditional and effective way to relieve acute bronchitis. When you drink an herbal tea, the active ingredients in the herbs are absorbed into your bloodstream and can reach the affected areas in your lungs. This is especially true for herbs like Eucalyptus globulus, which contains a compound called eucalyptol. Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant that can help ease congestion and make it easier to breathe.  Another herb that can be beneficial for acute bronchitis is Echinacea purpurea. This herb has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling in your airways and make it easier to breathe. It also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection that's causing your symptoms. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is another herb that can be helpful for acute bronchitis. It contains compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off infection.  Drinking herbal teas can bring many benefits to your life. For one thing, they can help you feel more comfortable and relieve your symptoms, making it easier to breathe and sleep. They can also help you recover faster from acute bronchitis, which means you can get back to your normal activities sooner. Additionally, herbal teas are a natural and gentle way to treat your symptoms, which can be especially important if you're taking medication or have other health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains cineole, a key bioactive constituent that helps relieve acute bronchitis symptoms. Cineole has expectorant properties, which help thin and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the bronchial tubes. Additionally, cineole's decongestant properties help open up airways, allowing for easier breathing. By combining these properties, eucalyptus globulus creams can provide quick relief from the congestion and cough associated with acute bronchitis.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Once the mixture is fully melted, remove it from heat and add the eucalyptus oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container or a tin, and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once the cream is solid, use it as needed to relieve symptoms of acute bronchitis. Store the cream in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, caffeic acid, chicoric acid, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to reduce the severity of acute bronchitis by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause the infection. The anti-inflammatory properties of alkylamides and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea creams also help to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Chicoric acid in Echinacea purpurea creams has antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, further contributing to its effectiveness in treating acute bronchitis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria and viruses, Echinacea purpurea creams helps to alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis, such as coughing and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea roots, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until melted and well combined.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea roots and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into a container. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Thymol helps to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause infections, while carvacrol's anti-inflammatory properties ease congestion and coughing associated with acute bronchitis. The antispasmodic properties of thymol also help to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe. The antimicrobial properties of carvacrol help to fight off the infection, reducing the severity of symptoms. By reducing inflammation and infection, Thymus vulgaris creams can help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis, such as coughing and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of Thymus vulgaris essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and add Thymus vulgaris essential oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and harden before use. Apply 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm acute bronchitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The analgesic and antipyretic properties of gingerol and shogaol also help alleviate the cough and fever associated with acute bronchitis. The demulcent properties of Zingiber officinale creams help soothe the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the bronchial tubes. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis by providing relief from congestion, cough, and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler at low heat. Stir until melted and smooth.
Add the dried Zingiber officinale root to the melted mixture and steep for 2 hours, then strain and discard the root.
Add the vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until solidified.
Apply a thin layer of the Zingiber officinale cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help relieve symptoms of acute bronchitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The rosmarinic acid in Rosmarinus officinalis creams has antioxidant properties, which combat oxidative stress and protect the lungs from damage. The antimicrobial properties of bornyl acetate and camphor in the cream also help control infections and promote healing in the respiratory tract. By reducing inflammation, combating infections, and protecting the lungs, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can provide relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax dissolves.
Add the distilled water and stir until well combined. Remove from heat.
Add the vitamin E oil and dried Rosmarinus officinalis to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Glycyrrhizin is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier. Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have antioxidant properties that can help combat oxidative stress and protect the lungs from damage. Phenolic acids, like ferulic acid, have expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis, such as coughing and shortness of breath.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of glycerin, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add glycerin, glycyrrhiza glabra powder, and vitamin E oil to the melted beeswax. Stir well.
Add distilled water to the mixture and stir until well combined. Continue heating until the mixture reaches 180°F to 190°F.
Remove from heat and let cool. Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains bioactive compounds like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off free radicals in the airways. Curcumin in particular has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes and molecules, such as COX-2 and NF-kB, that contribute to acute bronchitis. The antioxidant properties of curcuma longa can also help protect the airways from oxidative damage caused by pollutants and irritants. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis, such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax until the beeswax melts.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) and 1 tablespoon of ginger powder to the melted mixture.
Stir the mixture well and let it cool down to around 120°F (49°C).
Pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and solidify, and use as needed for acute bronchitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains a high concentration of essential oils, including menthol, menthone, and limonene. These active constituents work together to help alleviate the symptoms of acute bronchitis by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel. The menthol in Mentha x piperita creams has decongestant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways. The limonene also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing the bronchitis. By combining these properties, Mentha x piperita creams can help to ease the symptoms of acute bronchitis and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Stir in the peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil. Let cool until the mixture thickens.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely. Use as needed to relieve acute bronchitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two potent bioactive constituents with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the airways, making it an effective treatment for acute bronchitis. The decongestant and expectorant properties of Lavandula angustifolia creams also help to relieve congestion and cough, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus from the lungs. Additionally, the calming effects of the cream can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common symptoms of acute bronchitis. By using Lavandula angustifolia creams, you can help to alleviate the symptoms of acute bronchitis and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1/4 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the water in a saucepan over low heat. Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the flowers.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and coconut oil over low heat. Stir until smooth.
Combine the liquid from step 3 with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture. Stir until well combined. Pour into a container and let it cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis creams contains berberine, hydrastine, and palmarine as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. Berberine helps to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up, while hydrastine reduces inflammation in the bronchial tubes. Palmarine has antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying viral infections that cause acute bronchitis. By reducing inflammation and thinning mucus, Hydrastis canadensis creams can provide relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hydrastis canadensis root. Steep 2 tablespoons in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid and let it cool. Discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of a carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) with the cooled Hydrastis canadensis liquid.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the oil-liquid mixture. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool. Pour it into a container and let it solidify. Use as needed for acute bronchitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams contains flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils, which provide relief from acute bronchitis. Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the airways. Tannins in Sambucus nigra creams have a soothing effect, calming irritated mucous membranes and promoting a decrease in mucus production. The essential oils, including sambutol and sambunigrin, have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. By addressing inflammation and congestion, Sambucus nigra creams can provide symptomatic relief from acute bronchitis.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for acute bronchitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves. Wash them with water and pat dry.
Combine the Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally. This will help extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Let the mixture cool, then transfer it to a clean glass jar. Store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe a sore throat and calm a cough, which can be symptoms of acute bronchitis. Some herbal teas, like thyme and ginger tea, have anti-inflammatory properties that could reduce swelling in the airways. This might make it easier to breathe, but it's not a guarantee against developing acute bronchitis.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a soothing addition to your acute bronchitis treatment, it's essential to consume them in moderation. Drinking them daily might not be the best approach as some herbs can be too potent and potentially interact with underlying health conditions. It's best to limit them to a few times a week or as needed for relief.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with acute bronchitis, but it's not a quick fix. It may take a few days to notice some relief from symptoms like coughing and congestion. As the herbs like thyme and ginger work to soothe the airways, you might start feeling better within 3-7 days. This varies from person to person.
faq_time: For acute bronchitis, it's often best to drink herbal tea in the evening, right before bedtime. This can help soothe your throat and calm your cough, making it easier to sleep. As you relax, the tea's gentle warmth can ease your congestion, allowing you to breathe more comfortably through the night.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for acute bronchitis. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginseng, can slow down the breakdown of certain medications, making them more potent. This can cause side effects or reduce their effectiveness. The interaction depends on the specific tea and medication, so it's essential to be aware of the potential risks.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have acute bronchitis, it's a good idea to be careful with herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can help with symptoms, but others, like chamomile and licorice root, might not be safe for the baby. It's best to choose teas that are known to be safe for pregnancy, but always check the ingredients.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for acute bronchitis, but it's essential to choose ones that are safe for breastfeeding. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered okay, but others, like sage and thyme, may affect milk supply or baby's health. Always check the ingredients and look for "breastfeeding-friendly" labels.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams to Help Alleviate Acute Bronchitis in COPD Patients
